---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size45[
+ Case Study: CAFE Standards in the USA

+ Exploring the Role of Stakeholders in Policy-making
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Make sure to save time at end of class to discuss Thursday plan and the paper projects.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/03-1-CAFE_Standards_DoE.jpeg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

???

While CAFE standards are interesting and continue to be debated as a pollution control / energy independence tool, our focus today is on analyzing the role of stakeholders and advocacy in policy-making.

- Identify the key stakeholders and their competing framings of the problem

- What were the primary institutional settings? e.g. Where did they compete to shape policy and how?

- Strongest arguments on each side?

- Why did this policy ultimately get made? Can we tell from this?



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size35[
**Biden's New Rule**: Reach a projected industry-wide target of 161 grams CO2 /mile, or 40 miles per gallon value on fuel economy window stickers in 2026.

+ The final emission standards targets for 2023-2026 increase in stringency by between 5-10 percent in each model year.

+ The standards they replace increased in stringency by only 1.5 percent year over year.

+ By MY2026 the final emissions targets are 47 grams of CO2 /mile lower than the standards they replace.
]



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size70[**Thursday**]

.center[.size60[
Welcome to local stakeholders working on environmental policy!
]]

.size45[
+ Kane Sheek, Sierra Club - White River Group
    + Ready for 100 Springfield Community Organizer

+ ?

+ ?
]

???

We have a few goals for our discussion on Thursday

1. Familiarize ourselves with local environmental groups (stakeholders), and

2. Gather some info to help us with our paper projects.

<br>

Let's talk about the papers for a moment.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.center[.size55[**Solving a Local Environmental Problem**]]

<br>

.size40[
Paper 1: Present the basics of your problem

Paper 2: Analyze the complicating factors

Paper 3: Propose a policy to address the problem
]

???

Your papers this semester are designed to help you develop some expertise in a local environmental problem and attempt to outline a policy to address it.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle, slideblue

## Paper 1: Write a report introducing us to the specific, local environmental problem you intend to target this semester

.size35[
+ Why is this an "environmental" issue?

+ Who is this a "problem" for? Be specific.

+ What are the causes of the problem you want to focus on?

+ What data is available to allow us to measure any progress?
]

???

I'll post this description on the Moodle assignment.

The first paper requires you to select a local environmental problem and to begin to frame it as a policy problem we can solve.

<br>

THIS is the context I want you to bring to our class on Thursday.

Our local activists should have lots of information about the landscape of environmental problems in SW MO.

BUT I REALLY don't want you asking them to do the paper for you.

PLEASE come to class WITH at least a half an idea.

BEFORE class you should start looking around at the options.

Start thinking about what you might want to focus on this term.

### Questions on the assignment or the guests coming on Thursday?
